texto: When I was a kid, I loved 'The Curse of Frankenstein,' 'The Creeping Unknown,' 'X: The Unknown.' I love 'Forbidden Planet,' 'The Thing from Another World.' They were science fiction/horror movies, generally.

texto: Film buffs who don't live in Hollywood have a fantasy about what it's like to be a director. Movies and the people who make movies have such glamour associated with them. But the truth is, it's not like that. It's very different. It's hard work.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a John Carpenter: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Film buffs who don't live in Hollywood have a fantasy about what it's like to be a director. Movi…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, work, hard-work pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e hard-work.

texto: Monsters in movies are us, always us, one way or the other. They’re us with hats on. The zombies in George Romero’s movies are us. They’re hungry. Monsters are us, the dangerous parts of us. The part that wants to destroy. The part of us with the reptile brain. The part of us that’s vicious and cruel. We express these in our stories as these monsters out there.

texto: When somebody who makes movies for a living - either as an actor, writer, producer or director - lives to be a certain age, you have to admire them. It is an act of courage to make a film - a courage for which you are not prepared in the rest of life. It is very hard and very destructive. But we do it because we love it.
explicacao: Orientação direta de John Carpenter sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “When somebody who makes movies for a living - either as an actor, writer, producer or director - …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a vida, love, coragem, útil em momentos de vida e coragem.

It is an act of courage to make a film - a courage for which you are not prepared in the rest of life. It is very hard and very destructive. But we do it because we love it.

texto: I have two different categories of favorite films. One is the emotional favorites, which means these are generally films that I saw when I was a kid; anything you see in your formative years is more powerful, because it really stays with you forever. The second category is films that I saw while I was learning the craft of motion pictures.
